page: Microsoft Azure NC4as_T4_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCASv3_T4 family. It features an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring dedicated compute resource allocation.

This server configuration includes 28.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 7.0 GB of RAM per CPU core. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA T4 GPU featuring 15 GB of dedicated VRAM. For local storage, the instance is bundled with 180 GB of local disk space and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the NC4as_T4_v3 offers an entry-level GPU-accelerated hardware profile, balancing a dedicated AMD CPU configuration with a single NVIDIA T4 accelerator. This resource density makes it suitable for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the overhead of larger multi-GPU instances. Appropriate workloads include machine learning inference, video encoding, and remote visualization tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure NC4as_T4_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCASv3_T4 family, powered by an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs and 28.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration features a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 15 GB of VRAM, alongside 180 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the NC4as_T4_v3 delivers a balanced entry-level GPU profile, offering dedicated compute resources and hardware acceleration without the cost of high-end multi-GPU systems. This server is designed for workloads such as machine learning inference, light CUDA-based processing, video transcoding, and GPU-assisted database tasks.
